ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Cosmog'; depicted figure (small nebula-like creature, dark cosmic body, golden tuft, smiling face floating in starry void) matches the canonical Psychic-type Legendary/Nebula Pokemon, National Pokedex no. 789. Verified via card name, PokemonTCG.io metadata, Bulbapedia, and Serebii Pokedex entries.

trivia_bullets.text: Cosmog is National Pokedex no. 789, a Psychic-type Legendary Pokemon introduced in Generation VII (Sun/Moon, 2016), known as the Nebula Pokemon. At one time in Alola's history it was known only to the royal family — called 'the child of the stars.' [Serebii.net Pokedex 789; WebSearch snippet]

Cosmog nicknamed Nebby is the emotional core of Sun/Moon's story. Lillie smuggled it out of Aether Paradise three months before the game's events begin; it later evolves into Cosmoem (under stress from Lusamine's Ultra Wormhole machine) and then into either Solgaleo (Sun/Ultra Sun) or Lunala (Moon/Ultra Moon) — making Cosmog the pre-evolutionary stage of both cover legendaries. [Bulbapedia: Nebby; WebSearch snippet from Pokemon Fandom]

Flavor text on this card (from frontmatter capture, artist Mizue): "Even though its helpless, gaseous body can be blown away by the slightest breeze, it doesn't seem to care." — Consistent with Cosmog's in-game characterization as carefree and oblivious to its own cosmic importance. [PokemonTCG.io: sm12-99 flavorText field]

This card's sole attack is Ascension: search the deck for a card that evolves from Cosmog (Cosmoem) and put it onto this Pokemon to evolve it, then shuffle the deck. A one-card accelerated evolution pipeline reflecting Cosmog's narrative role as the beginning of an unavoidable transformation. [PokemonTCG.io: sm12-99 oracle_text; frontmatter capture]
